chapters.name: Circles
chapters.description: Tangents and related theorems on circles.
chapters.lessonSummary: Use tangent properties and equal tangent lengths from external points.

questions.prompt: From an external point P to a circle with center O, OP = 13 cm and radius = 5 cm. Find the tangent length PT.
questions.explanation: Use PT^2 = OP^2 - r^2.

questions.prompt: From an external point P to a circle with center O, OP = 10 cm and radius = 6 cm. Find the tangent length PT.
questions.explanation: Use PT^2 = OP^2 - r^2.

questions.prompt: From an external point P to a circle with center O, OP = 17 cm and radius = 8 cm. Find the tangent length PT.
questions.explanation: Use PT^2 = OP^2 - r^2.

questions.prompt: From an external point P to a circle with center O, OP = 25 cm and radius = 7 cm. Find the tangent length PT.
questions.explanation: Use PT^2 = OP^2 - r^2.

questions.prompt: From an external point P to a circle with center O, OP = 15 cm and radius = 9 cm. Find the tangent length PT.
questions.explanation: Use PT^2 = OP^2 - r^2.

questions.prompt: From an external point P to a circle with center O, OP = 20 cm and radius = 16 cm. Find the tangent length PT.
questions.explanation: Use PT^2 = OP^2 - r^2.

questions.prompt: A tangent is perpendicular to the radius at the point of contact.
questions.explanation: Use the standard properties of tangents and circles.

questions.prompt: Two tangents drawn from an external point are always unequal.
questions.explanation: Use the standard properties of tangents and circles.

questions.prompt: A circle can have exactly one tangent at a point on the circle.
questions.explanation: Use the standard properties of tangents and circles.

questions.prompt: A secant touches the circle at exactly one point.
questions.explanation: Use the standard properties of tangents and circles.

questions.prompt: The line joining the center to the point of contact is perpendicular to the tangent.
questions.explanation: Use the standard properties of tangents and circles.

questions.prompt: Every chord is a tangent.
questions.explanation: Use the standard properties of tangents and circles.

questions.prompt: The tangent at any point of a circle is ____ to the radius through the point of contact.
questions.explanation: This is the fundamental tangent property.

questions.prompt: Tangents drawn from an external point to a circle are ____ in length.
questions.explanation: This is a basic theorem.

questions.prompt: A radius through the point of contact meets the tangent at 90 degrees.
questions.explanation: That is the tangent-radius theorem.

questions.prompt: A tangent can intersect a circle at two points.
questions.explanation: A tangent touches at exactly one point.

questions.prompt: If PA and PB are tangents from P, then PA = ____.
questions.explanation: Tangents from an external point are equal.

questions.prompt: A tangent segment lies outside the circle except at the point of contact.
questions.explanation: It touches the circle only once.

questions.prompt: If PA and PB are tangents from P to a circle and PA = 11 cm, find PB.
questions.explanation: Tangents from an external point are equal.

questions.prompt: A tangent from P to a circle of radius 9 cm has OP = 15 cm. Find PT.
questions.explanation: Use PT^2 = OP^2 - r^2.

questions.prompt: A student draws two tangents from an external point and gets lengths 7 cm and 9 cm. Is the drawing correct for the same circle? Answer True or False.
questions.explanation: Tangents from the same external point must be equal.

questions.prompt: A radius of 10 cm is drawn to the point of contact of a tangent. What angle does it make with the tangent?
questions.explanation: The radius is perpendicular to the tangent.

questions.prompt: If the radius at the point of contact is 6 cm, does that change the angle between radius and tangent? Answer Yes or No.
questions.explanation: The angle remains 90 degrees for every tangent.

questions.prompt: From point P, two tangents touch a circle at A and B. If the perimeter of triangle PAB is 26 cm and PA = 5 cm, what is AB?
questions.explanation: Since PA = PB = 5, AB = 26 - 10.

questions.prompt: If OP = 26 cm and radius = 10 cm, find the tangent length PT.
questions.explanation: Use the Pythagorean relation with tangent and radius.

questions.prompt: How many tangents can be drawn at a point on a circle?
questions.explanation: Exactly one tangent can be drawn at a point on the circle.

questions.prompt: A tangent touches a circle at exactly ____ point.
questions.explanation: That is what distinguishes a tangent from a secant.

questions.prompt: If PA and PB are tangents from P, triangle PAB is always isosceles.
questions.explanation: Two equal sides are formed by PA and PB.

questions.prompt: The tangent length is 24 cm and radius is 7 cm. What is OP?
questions.explanation: Use OP^2 = PT^2 + r^2.

questions.prompt: If OP is joined to the point of contact T, what kind of triangle is OPT when PT is a tangent?
questions.explanation: The angle at T is 90 degrees.
